name: Production Issue Fixes Knowledge Pack
description: Installable knowledge pack of original, concise production-incident fix patterns for the LocalAIFactory deployment estate (ASP.NET Core / IIS / SQL Server Express / EF Core on Windows) plus common third-party integration failures (WordPress, Odoo, ERPNext/Frappe, WooCommerce, Magento, Drupal, PrestaShop, Joomla, OpenCart, Keycloak/OIDC, Entra ID, Salesforce, ServiceNow, SFTP/SMTP), platform and security concerns (CORS/SameSite cookies, forwarded headers behind a reverse proxy, IIS request/upload size limits and app-pool recycling, Data Protection key persistence, OAuth2 token refresh, API rate-limiting and webhooks, EF migration concurrency, SQL connection-pool exhaustion and deadlocks, TLS cipher/protocol and certificate-chain issues, HSTS/mixed-content, supply-chain/SBOM, backup RPO/RTO), observability exporter configuration (Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry), and optional-component issues (Ollama, Qdrant, OCR/PDF). Each item encodes symptoms, root cause, a diagnostic command, a known fix, a prevention rule, how LocalAIFactory should detect it, how it should fix or warn, and a confidence score. Several items capture firsthand findings from this project (clone-retry 'destination exists', ANCM absent on a fresh IIS host, Invoke-WebRequest not sending credentials over HTTP, the appcmd attribute-path quirk, and the GroupBy(_ => 1) / large-text page-hang lesson).
license: Original professional troubleshooting summaries authored for LocalAIFactory. Free to deploy and modify within the application. No third-party support answer, vendor documentation, or copyrighted text is reproduced verbatim. Community patterns are framed honestly with confidence levels; no vendor certification is asserted.
legalLimitations: This pack contains ORIGINAL practical troubleshooting summaries only. It does NOT reproduce vendor documentation, support-forum answers, or any proprietary/official text verbatim, and it does not assert official certification or a compliance claim. Diagnostic commands and fixes are general patterns that must be validated against the deployed product versions; framework, server, and third-party behavior changes between versions. Confidence scores are author estimates, not guarantees. Items derived from public source families are attributed via source-registry.json; firsthand items are observations from this project and are marked as such.
sourcePolicy: Original professional summaries and practical patterns only. No proprietary/official text copied verbatim. Generic public source families are registered in source-registry.json for governance and attribution (references only, no copyrighted text). Every item carries an explicit limitation note and a confidence score.
